verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a secreted glycoprotein without transmembrane domains, GPI anchoring, or covalent surface linkages. It transiently binds sperm and egg surfaces via reversible non-covalent interactions with putative receptors, but does not itself reach or anchor to the plasma membrane outer face via its own mechanism. The protein equilibrates between secreted and receptor-bound pools and is released during capacitation. No therapeutic modality would target CRISP1 as a cell-surface antigen; it is a secreted ligand only.
